14.3.5 Upper Limit Setting Monitor for Maximum Motor Speed/ Upper
       Limit Setting for Encoder Output Resolution (Un010)
       You can use Un010 to monitor the upper limit setting for the maximum motor speed or the upper limit setting for
       the encoder output resolution.
       You can monitor the upper limit of Pn281 (Encoder Output Resolution) for the current Pn385 (Maximum Motor
       Speed), or you can monitor the upper limit of the maximum motor speed setting for the current encoder output
       resolution setting.
       Select which signal to monitor with Pn080 = n.X☐☐☐ (Calculation Method for Maximum Speed or Encoder
       Output Pulses).
       • If Pn080 = n.0☐☐☐, Pn281 (Encoder Output Resolution) that can be set is displayed.
       • If Pn080 = n.1☐☐☐, Pn385 (Maximum Motor Speed) that can be set is displayed in mm/s.



14.3.6 Polarity Sensor Signal Monitor (Un011)
       You can use Un011 to monitor the signal pattern of the polarity sensor.
       Press the [DATA/SHIFT] key for approximately one second to display the polarity sensor signal pattern.

14.4 Utility Function (Fn☐☐☐) Operations on the Panel Operator


14.4 Utility Function (Fn☐☐☐) Operations on the Panel
     Operator
           Utility functions are used to set up and tune the SERVOPACK.
           The panel operator displays numbers beginning with “Fn.”
           Display Example: Origin Search



           The operating procedures from the panel operator are described here. Refer to the descriptions of individual util-
           ity functions for preparations and related parameters.
           Refer to the following section for details on utility functions.
              16.2.1 Corresponding SERVOPACK Utility Function Names on page 791



14.4.1 Display Alarm History (Fn000)
           Refer to the following section for information on this utility function other than the procedure.
              13.2.4 Displaying the Alarm History on page 657

                        Panel Display after
            Step                                           Keys                                           Operation
                           Operation



                                                                           Press the [MODE/SET] key to enter Utility Function Mode. If “Fn000”
              1
                                                                           is not displayed, press the [UP] key or [DOWN] key to display “Fn000”.




                                                                           Press the [DATA/SHIFT] key for approximately one second. The most
              2
                                                                           recent alarm will be displayed.

                                                                           Press the [DOWN] key to display the next older alarm. Press the [UP]
                                                                           key to display the next newer alarm. The higher the far-left segment is,
              3                                                            the older the alarm is.
                                                                           Refer to the following section for information on alarms.
                                                                                13.2.1 List of Alarms on page 624

                                                                           Press the [DATA/SHIFT] key. The lower four digits of the time stamp
              4
                                                                           will be displayed.


                                                                           Press the [DATA/SHIFT] key. The middle four digits of the time stamp
              5
                                                                           will be displayed.


                                                                           Press the [DATA/SHIFT] key. The upper two digits of the time stamp
              6
                                                                           will be displayed.


                                                                           Press the [DATA/SHIFT] key. The alarm number will flash on the
              7
                                                                           display.


                                                                           Press the [DATA/SHIFT] key again for approximately one second to
              8
                                                                           return the display to “Fn000”.

               Information   Time Stamp
                             A time stamp gives the total operating time to the point at which the alarm occurred in increments of 100 ms from when
                             the control power and main circuit power were turned ON. For 24-hour, 365-day operation, measurements are possible
                             for approximately 13 years.
           Time Stamp Display Example



690
                                                     14.4 Utility Function (Fn☐☐☐) Operations on the Panel Operator

       If 36000 is displayed, 3600000 ms = 3600 s = 60 min = 1 h. Therefore, the total operating time in hours is 1
       hour.



14.4.2 Jog (Fn002)
       Refer to the following section for information on this utility function other than the procedure.
          7.3 Trial Operation for the Servomotor without a Load on page 353

        Step      Panel Display after               Keys                                        Operation
                     Operation


          1                                                        Press the [MODE/SET] key to enter Utility Function Mode.



          2                                                        Press the [UP] key or [DOWN] key to display “Fn002”.


                                                                   Press the [DATA/SHIFT] key for approximately one second. The display
          3
                                                                   shown at the left will appear.


          4                                                        Press the [MODE/SET] key to turn ON the servo.


                                                                   The servomotor will operate at the speed set in Pn304 or Pn383 while
          5                                                        the [UP] key (for forward operation) or [DOWN] key (for reverse opera-
                                                                   tion) is pressed.

                                                                   Press the [MODE/SET] key to turn OFF the servo.
                                                                   Additional Information
          6
                                                                   You can turn OFF the servo by pressing the [DATA/SHIFT] key for
                                                                   approximately one second.

                                                                   Press the [DATA/SHIFT] key for approximately one second to return the
          7
                                                                   display to “Fn002”.

          8    After you finish jogging operation, turn the power to the SERVOPACK OFF and ON again.

14.4 Utility Function (Fn☐☐☐) Operations on the Panel Operator

                                                                                                                   Continued from previous page.

                      Panel Display after
            Step                                        Keys                                          Operation
                         Operation

                                                                        Press the [UP] key to operate the servomotor in the forward direction.
                                                                        Press the [DOWN] key to operate the servomotor in the reverse
                                                                        direction.
                                                                        The rotation direction of the servomotor changes according to the setting
                                                                        of Pn000 = n.☐☐☐X as follows.
                                                                        Rotary Servomotors
                                                                        • n.□□□0
                                                                          [UP] key: CCW, [DOWN] key: CW
                                                                        • n.□□□1
                                                                          [UP] key: CW, [DOWN] key: CCW
                                                                        Note:
              5                                                         This is the direction when viewed from the load side of the servomotor.
                                                                        Linear Servomotors
                                                                        • n.□□□0
                                                                          [UP] key: Linear encoder counts up, [DOWN] key: Linear encoder
                                                                          counts down
                                                                        • n.□□□1
                                                                          [UP] key: Linear encoder counts down, [DOWN] key: Linear encoder
                                                                          counts up
                                                                        Note:
                                                                        The direction in which the linear encoder counts up is the forward direc-
                                                                        tion. Refer to the following sections for details.
                                                                            5.6 Motor Direction Setting on page 191


              6                                                         When the servomotor origin search has been completed, the display will
                                                          –
                                                                        flash. At this time, the servomotor is servo-locked at encoder phase C.
                           (Flashing)

                                                                        Press the [DATA/SHIFT] key for approximately one second to return the
              7
                                                                        display to “Fn003”.

              6.16.2 Manual Adjustment on page 338

                      Panel Display after
            Step                                     Keys                                        Operation
                         Operation


              1                                                   Press the [MODE/SET] key to enter Utility Function Mode.



              2                                                   Press the [UP] key or [DOWN] key to display “Fn00F.”


                                                                  First you adjust the offset for phase U (Cu1-o).
              3                                                   Press the [DATA/SHIFT] key for approximately one second. The display
                                                                  shown at the left will appear.

                                                                  Press the [DATA/SHIFT] key. The amount of offset for phase U will be
              4
                                                                  displayed.

                                                                                                                     Continued on next page.




698
                                                             14.4 Utility Function (Fn☐☐☐) Operations on the Panel Operator

                                                                                                                       Continued from previous page.

                        Panel Display after
        Step                                                Keys                                           Operation
                           Operation

                                                                            Press the [UP] key or [DOWN] key to change the amount of offset.
                                                                            Change the setting by approximately 10 units at a time in the direction
            5                                                               that reduces the torque ripple to find the value that minimizes the torque
                                                                            ripple, and set that value.
                                                                            Adjustment range: -512 to +511

                                                                            Press the [DATA/SHIFT] key. The display shown on the left will appear
            6
                                                                            again.

                                                                            Next you adjust the offset for phase V (Cu2-o).
            7                                                               Press the [MODE/SET] key for approximately one second. The display
                                                                            shown at the left will appear.

                                                                            Press the [DATA/SHIFT] key. The amount of offset for phase V will be
            8
                                                                            displayed.

                                                                            Press the [UP] key or [DOWN] key to change the amount of offset.
                                                                            In the same way as for the phase-U adjustment, change the setting by
                                                                            approximately 10 units at a time in the direction that reduces the torque
            9
                                                                            ripple to find the value that minimizes the torque ripple, and set that
                                                                            value.
                                                                            Adjustment range: -512 to +511

                                                                            Press the [DATA/SHIFT] key for approximately one second. “Cu2-o”
            10
                                                                            will be displayed, and then “Fn00F” will be displayed again.

            11      Reduce the amount by which you change the offsets each time and repeat steps 3 to 10 *1 to fine-tune the amounts of offset.

       *1        Examples of the Amount of Change to Adjust Offsets
                 • First time: 10 units at a time
                 • Second time: 5 units at a time
                 • Third time: 1 unit at a time
                 The above values are guidelines. Vary the amount of change and the number of repetitions according to your system.

                                                                   shown on the left will appear.

                                                                   Continue pressing the [UP] key until the display shown on the left
                                                                   appears.
          4                                                        Note:
                                                                   If you make a mistake during key operations, “no_oP” will flash on the
                                                                   display for approximately one second.

                                                                   Press the [MODE/SET] key. The panel display will go blank and then
          5                                                        will change to the same initial status display as when the power is turned
                                                                   ON.

14.4.27 Tuning-less Level Setting (Fn200)
           Refer to the following section for information on this utility function other than the procedure.
              8.4 Tuning-less Function on page 388

                      Panel Display after
            Step                                     Keys                                         Operation
                         Operation


              1                                                   Press the [MODE/SET] key to enter Utility Function Mode.



              2                                                   Press the [UP] key or [DOWN] key to display “Fn200.”


                                                                  Press the [DATA/SHIFT] key for approximately one second. The load
                                                                  level setting display for the tuning-less levels will appear.
                                                                  Note:
              3                                                   If the response waveform shows overshooting, if the load moment of
                                                                  inertia exceeds the allowable level (i.e., outside the scope of product
                                                                  warranty), or if the mass ratio is 30 or higher (i.e., outside the scope of
                                                                  product warranty), press the [UP] key and change the load level to 2.


              4                                                   Press the [MODE/SET] key. The response level setting display for the
                                                                  tuning-less levels will appear.

                                                                  Press the [UP] key or [DOWN] key to set the response level.
                                                                  Set the response level to between 0 and 7. The larger the value is, the
                                                                  higher the gain is and the better response performance will be. (The
                                                                  default setting is 4.)
              5                                                   Note:
                                                                  Vibration may occur if the response level is too high. Lower the response
                                                                  level if vibration occurs. If high-frequency noise is generated, press the
                                                                  [DATA/SHIFT] key to automatically set a notch filter for the vibration
                                                                  frequency.


              6                                                   Press the [MODE/SET] key. “donE” will flash for approximately one
                                                                  second and then “L 4” will be displayed.


                                                                  Press the [DATA/SHIFT] key for approximately one second to return the
              7
                                                                  display to “Fn200”.

14.4.30 One-Parameter Tuning (Fn203)
       Refer to the following section for information on this utility function other than the procedure.
          8.9 Custom Tuning on page 450

                  Panel Display after
        Step                                     Keys                                          Operation
                     Operation


          1                                                   Press the [MODE/SET] key to enter Utility Function Mode.



          2                                                   Press the [UP] key or [DOWN] key to display “Fn203”.


                                                              Press the [DATA/SHIFT] key for approximately one second. The display
          3
                                                              shown at the left will appear.

                                                              Press the [UP] key or [DOWN] key to enter Tuning Mode.
                                                              Tuning Mode (Strength of Tuning Setting)
                                                              0: Performs tuning giving priority to stability.
          4
                                                              1: Performs tuning giving priority to response.
                                                              Note:
                                                              The rigidity type is always 2.

                                                              If the servo is OFF (i.e., if power is not supplied to the servomotor),
          5                                       –           input the /S-ON (Servo ON) from the host controller.
                                                              If the servo is ON, go to step 6.

                                                              Press the [DATA/SHIFT] key for less than one second. The one-parame-
          6
                                                              ter gain will be displayed as shown on the left.




                                                                                                                                              Panel Displays and Panel Operator Procedures
                                                              Press the [UP] key or [DOWN] key to change the one-parameter gain
                                                              value and change the actual servo gains (Pn100, Pn101, Pn102, and
          7                                                   Pn401) at the same time.
                                                              You end this tuning function when you decide that the response is
                                                              satisfactory.

                                                              Press the [MODE/SET] key to save the four calculated gains to the
                                                              parameters. When tuning has been completed, “donE” will flash on the
          8                                                   display, and then the display shown on the left will appear again.
                                                              Note:
                                                              To end operation without saving the calculated gains, go to step 9.


          9                                                   Press the [DATA/SHIFT] key for approximately one second to return the
                                                              display to “Fn203”.

14.4.33 Easy FFT (Fn206)
           Refer to the following section for information on this utility function other than the procedure.
              8.17.2 Easy FFT on page 536

                      Panel Display after
            Step                                     Keys                                        Operation
                         Operation


              1                                                   Press the [MODE/SET] key to enter Utility Function Mode.



              2                                                   Press the [UP] key or [DOWN] key to display Fn206.



                                                                  Press the [DATA/SHIFT] key for approximately one second. The display
              3                                                   shown on the left will appear and the panel operator will enter Reference
                       (Reference amplitude                       Amplitude Setting Mode.
                              setting)

                                                                  Press the [UP] key or [DOWN] key to set the reference amplitude.
                                                                  Reference amplitude setting range: 1 to 800
                                                                  Note:
                                                                  1. If you are setting Easy FFT for the first time, do not change the
              4                                                      reference amplitude setting, but rather start from the default value of
                                                                     15. Although increasing the reference amplitude will increase the
                                                                     detection accuracy, the vibration and noise in the machine will
                                                                     increase momentarily. Increase the reference amplitude a little at a
                                                                     time and observe the results.
                                                                  2. The set reference amplitude will be stored in Pn456.


              5                                                   Press the [DATA/SHIFT] key for approximately one second. The ready
                                                                  to operate status will be entered.
                     (Ready to operate status)

                                                                  Press the [MODE/SET] key to turn ON the servo.
                                                                  Additional Information
              6
                                                                  Press the [MODE/SET] key again to turn OFF the servo. Return to step
                                                                  5.

                                                                  While the servo is ON, press the [UP] (forward) key or the [DOWN]
                                                                  (reverse) key. The servomotor will automatically perform round-trip
                                                                  operation, moving in forward and reverse several times for 1/4th of a
                                                                  rotation for a rotary servomotor and 10 mm or less for a linear servomo-
                            (Flashing)                            tor. The servomotor performs this operation for approximately 2 sec-
                                                                  onds. During this operation, the display shown on the left will flash.
              7
                                                                  Note:
                                                                  1. Press the [MODE/SET] key to cancel the operation. Return to step
                                                                     5.
                                                                  2. The servomotor will move slightly. Also at the same time, the servo-
                       Slight movement in
                                                                     motor will emit noise. To ensure safety, do not approach or enter the
                           servomotor                                range of machine motion.
                                                                                                                    Continued on next page.

                                             14.4 Utility Function (Fn☐☐☐) Operations on the Panel Operator

                                                                                                          Continued from previous page.

          Panel Display after
Step                                       Keys                                               Operation
             Operation

                                                          If detection is completed normally, “E_FFt” will stop flashing and the
                                                          detected resonance frequency will be displayed. If detection fails,
                                                          “F——” will be displayed.
                                                          To set the results, go to step 9.
                                                          To monitor the resonance frequency without setting the detection result,
                                                          press the [DATA/SHIFT] key for approximately one second and return
                                                          to step 2.
 8                                           –            IMPORTANT
         (Detection result display
                                                          If the operation ended normally but it took two seconds or longer, the
                example)
                                                          detection accuracy may not be sufficient. Increase the reference ampli-
                                                          tude to a value higher than 15 to increase the detection accuracy.
                                                          Although increasing the reference amplitude will increase the detection
                                                          accuracy, the vibration and noise in the machine will increase momentar-
                                                          ily. Increase the reference amplitude a little at a time and observe the
                                                          results.

                                                          Press the [MODE/SET] key. The optimum notch filter for the detected
                                                          resonance frequency will automatically be set. If the notch filter is set
                                                          correctly, “donE” will flash on the display, and then the display shown on
                                                          the left will appear.
                                                          When Pn408 is set to n.□□□1 (enable first stage notch filter), Pn40C
                                                          (Second Stage Notch Filter Frequency) is set automatically.
 9
                                                          Press the [MODE/SET] key to return to step 5.
                                                          Note:
                                                          1. When Pn408 is set to n.□1□1 (enable first and second stage notch fil-
                                                             ters), notch filter frequencies are not set.
                                                          2. If the frequency detected by this function is not to be used, set
                                                             Pn408 to n.□□□0 (disable notch filter).


10                                                        Press the [DATA/SHIFT] key for approximately one second to return the
                                                          display to Fn206.

 11    Turn the power to the SERVOPACK OFF and ON again after you finish executing Easy FFT.

                                                                                                                            711
15.1 Interpreting the Parameter Lists


15.1 Interpreting the Parameter Lists




            No.               Item                                                          Meaning

                                        Indicates the types of servomotors to which the parameter applies.
                                        • All: The parameter is used for both rotary servomotors and linear servomotors.
                                        • Rotary: The parameter is used for only rotary servomotors.
                                        • Linear: The parameter is used for only linear servomotors.
             (1)   Applicable Motors    If this item differs by digit, it is added to the digit table.
                                        Rotary servomotor terms are used for parameters that are applicable to all servomotors. If you are using
                                        a linear servomotor, you need to interpret the terms accordingly. Refer to the following sections for
                                        details.
                                            i.5.2 Differences in Terms for Rotary Servomotors and Linear Servomotors on page 28

                                        Indicates when a change to the parameter will be effective. “After restart” indicates parameters that will
                                        be effective after one of the following is executed.
             (2)   When Enabled         • The power is turned OFF and ON again.
                                        • A software reset is executed.
                                        If this item differs by digit, it is added to the digit table.

                                        There are the following two classifications.
                                        • Setup
             (3)   Classification       • Tuning
                                        Refer to the following sections for details.
                                            5.1.1 Parameter Classification on page 176

             (4)   Default Setting      The default setting.

                                        If there are differences in the parameters for rotary servomotor and linear servomotor, information is
                                        provided for both.
             (5)   Digit Name
                                        • Top row: For rotary servomotors
                                        • Bottom row: For linear servomotors

                                               : A parameter that can be used in speed control.
                                               : A parameter that can be used in position control.
                                             : A parameter that can be used in torque control. "Torque" is used even for linear servomotor
             (6)   Control Mode         parameters.
                                        Grayed-out icons (        ,      ,        ) indicate parameters that cannot be used in the corresponding con-
                                        trol method.
                                        For parameters for numeric settings, this item is added next to the parameter name.
                                        For parameters for selecting functions, this item is added to each digit in the table.

   n.□□□X     Gain Switching Selection                                                                           Speed     Pos   Trq

         0
    Default   The gain is switched manually with the /G-SEL (Gain Selection Input) signal.

         0

         2    /NEAR (Near Output) signal turns ON.

         3    /NEAR (Near Output) signal turns OFF.

         4    Position reference filter output is 0 and reference pulse input is OFF.

                  2    Two-phase pulse trains with 90° phase differential (phase A and phase B) ×1, positive logic                            267

                  3    Two-phase pulse trains with 90° phase differential (phase A and phase B) ×2, positive logic                            267

                  4    Two-phase pulse trains with 90° phase differential (phase A and phase B) ×4, positive logic                            267

                  5    Sign and pulse train, negative logic.                                                                                  267

                  6    CW and CCW pulse trains, negative logic                                                                                267
           n.□□X□      Clear Signal Form                                                                             Speed   Pos     Trq       –

                  0
                       Clear position deviation when the signal is at high level.                                                             269
             Default

                  1    Clear position deviation on the rising edge of the signal.                                                             269

                  2    Clear position deviation when the signal is at low level.                                                              269

                  3    Clear position deviation on the falling edge of the signal.                                                            269
           n.□X□□      Clear Operation                                                                               Speed   Pos     Trq       –

                  0
                       Clear position deviation at a base block (at servo OFF or when alarm occurs).                                          269
             Default

                  1    Do not clear position error (cleared only with CLR (Clear Position Deviation) signal).                                 269

                  2    Clear position deviation when an alarm occurs.                                                                         269
           n.X□□□      Filter Selection                                                                              Speed   Pos     Trq       –

                  0
                       Use the reference input filter 1 for a line-driver signal. (1 Mpps max.)                                               267
             Default

                  1    Use the reference input filter for an open-collector signal. (200 kpps max.)                                           267

                  2    Use the reference input filter 2 for a line-driver signal. (1 to 4 Mpps)                                               267

16.1 Examples of Connections to Host Controllers


16.1 Examples of Connections to Host Controllers
           This section provides examples of connections to host controllers.



16.1.1 Example of Connections to MP2000/MP3000-Series SVA-01
       Motion Module




786
                                                                              16.1 Examples of Connections to Host Controllers

      Note:
      1. Cables to connect the SERVOPACK to the MP2000/MP3000 are available from Yaskawa.
         For details, refer to the manual for the machine controller.
      2. Only signals that are applicable to the MP2000/MP3000-series SVA-01 motion module and the SERVOPACK are shown in the diagram.
      3. The main circuit power supply for the SERVOPACK in this connection example is three-phase 200 VAC.
      4. Incorrect wiring may damage the machine controller or SERVOPACK. Wire all connections carefully.
      5. Do not wire any unused signal lines (i.e., leave them open).
      6. The above wiring diagram shows the connections for only one axis. If you will use other axes, make connections to the SERVOPACK in
         the same way.
      7. All normally closed input terminals that are not used at the machine controller’s I/O connector section must be connected at the
         connector.
      8. Set the parameters so that the servo can be turned ON and OFF with the /S-ON (Servo ON) signal.
      9. The SERVOPACK provides safety functions to protect people from the hazardous operation of the moving parts of the machine. In order
         to use the safety functions, the required circuits must be configured for CN8. If the safety functions will not be used, leave the enclosed
         safety jumper connector connected to the SERVOPACK (CN8). Refer to the following chapter for details.
              12 Safety Functions on page 605




16.1.2 Example of Connections to Yokogawa Electric’s F3YP2☐-0P
       Positioning Module for Position Control




      *1      Indicates shielded twisted-pair cable.
      *2      The pulse output from Yokogawa Electric’s F3YP2☐-0P positioning module uses negative logic, so the positive and negative connec-
              tions are reversed.
      Note:
      1.   Only signals that are applicable to the SERVOPACK and Yokogawa Electric’s F3YP2☐-0P positioning module are shown in the diagram.
      2.   Incorrect wiring may damage the positioning module or SERVOPACK. Wire all connections carefully.
      3.   Do not wire any unused signal lines (i.e., leave them open).



                                                                                                                                                       Appendices
      4.   The above wiring diagram shows the connections for only one axis. If you will use other axes, performing wiring to the SERVOPACK in
           the same way.




                                                                                                                                                          16

                                                                                                                                                787
16.1 Examples of Connections to Host Controllers


16.1.3 Example of Connections to Yokogawa Electric’s F3NC3☐-0N
       Positioning Module for Position Control




           *1      Indicates shielded twisted-pair cable.
           *2      The pulse output from Yokogawa Electric’s F3NC3☐-0N positioning module uses negative logic, so the positive and negative connec-
                   tions are reversed.
           *3      Connect these when using an absolute encoder.
           Note:
           1. Only signals that are applicable to the SERVOPACK and Yokogawa Electric’s F3NC3☐-0N positioning module are shown in the
              diagram.
           2. Incorrect wiring may damage the positioning module or SERVOPACK. Wire all connections carefully.
           3. Do not wire any unused signal lines (i.e., leave them open).
           4. The above wiring diagram shows the connections for only one axis. If you will use other axes, performing wiring to the SERVOPACK in
              the same way.




788
                                                                              16.1 Examples of Connections to Host Controllers


16.1.4 Example of Connections to an OMRON Position Control Unit




      *1     The ALM (Servo Alarm) signal is output for up to 10 seconds when the power supply is turned ON. Take this into consideration when
             designing the power ON sequence. Also, use the ALM signal to actuate the alarm detection relay (1Ry) to stop the main circuit power
             supply to the SERVOPACK.
      *2     Set Pn200 to n.□□□1 (CW and CCW pulse trains with positive logic).
      *3     Connect the shielded wire to the connector shell.

      *4         represents a shielded twisted-pair cable.
      Note:
      1. Only the signals that are applicable to the SERVOPACK and the OMRON position control unit are shown in the diagram.
      2. The main circuit power supply for the SERVOPACK in this connection example is three-phase 200 VAC.
      3. Incorrect wiring may damage the position control unit or SERVOPACK. Wire all connections carefully.
      4. Do not wire any unused signal lines (i.e., leave them open).
      5. The above wiring diagram shows the connections for only the X axis. If you will use other axes, make connections to the SERVOPACK
         in the same way.
      6. All normally closed input terminals that are not used at the position control unit’s I/O connector section must be connected at the
         connector.
      7. Set the parameters so that the servo can be turned ON and OFF with the /S-ON (Servo ON Input) signal.
      8. The SERVOPACK provides safety functions to protect people from the hazardous operation of the moving parts of the machine. In order
         to use the safety functions, the required circuits must be configured for CN8. If the safety functions will not be used, leave the enclosed
         safety jumper connector connected to the SERVOPACK (CN8). Refer to the following chapter for details.
              12 Safety Functions on page 605




                                                                                                                                                       Appendices



                                                                                                                                                          16

                                                                                                                                                789
16.1 Examples of Connections to Host Controllers


16.1.5 Example of Connection to Mitsubishi’s QD75D☐ Positioning
       Module for Position Control




           *1     The ALM (Servo Alarm) signal is output for up to 10 seconds when the power supply is turned ON. Take this into consideration when
                  designing the power ON sequence. Also, use the ALM signal to actuate the alarm detection relay (1Ry) to stop the main circuit power
                  supply to the SERVOPACK.

           *2        represents a shielded twisted-pair cable.
           *3     Connect the shielded wire to the connector shell.
           Note:
           1. Only the signals that are applicable to the SERVOPACK and Mitsubishi Electric’s QD75D☐ positioning module are shown in the
              diagram.
           2. The main circuit power supply for the SERVOPACK in this connection example is three-phase 200 VAC.
           3. Incorrect wiring may damage the positioning module or SERVOPACK. Wire all connections carefully.
           4. Do not wire any unused signal lines (i.e., leave them open).
           5. The above wiring diagram shows the connections for only one axis. If you will use other axes, make connections to the SERVOPACK in
              the same way.
           6. All normally closed input terminals that are not used at the positioning module’s I/O connector section must be connected at the
              connector.
           7. Set the parameters so that the servo can be turned ON and OFF with the /S-ON (Servo ON Input) signal.
           8. The SERVOPACK provides safety functions to protect people from the hazardous operation of the moving parts of the machine. In order
              to use the safety functions, the required circuits must be configured for CN8. If the safety functions will not be used, leave the enclosed
              safety jumper connector connected to the SERVOPACK (CN8). Refer to the following chapter for details.
                   12 Safety Functions on page 605

*1     You can use Un010 to monitor the upper limit setting for the maximum motor speed or the upper limit setting for the encoder output
       resolution.
       You can monitor the upper limit of Pn281 (Encoder Output Resolution) for the current Pn385 (Maximum Motor Speed), or you can
       monitor the upper limit of the maximum motor speed setting for the current encoder output resolution setting.
       Select which signal to monitor with Pn080 = n.X□□□ (Calculation Method for Maximum Speed or Encoder Output Pulses).
       • If Pn080 = n.0□□□, Pn281 (Encoder Output Resolution) that can be set is displayed.
       • If Pn080 = n.1□□□, Pn385 (Maximum Motor Speed) that can be set is displayed in mm/s.
*2     When an encoder other than a compact batteryless absolute encoder is connected, do not reference the monitor value because it will
       be indefinite.
*3     When a compact batteryless absolute encoder is connected, do not reference the monitor value because it will be indefinite.

                            In the event that the end user of this product is to be the military and said product is to be employed
                            in any weapons systems or the manufacture thereof, the export will fall under the relevant regulations
                            as stipulated in the Foreign Exchange and Foreign Trade Act. Therefore, be sure to follow all
                            procedures and submit all relevant documentation according to any and all rules, regulations and
                            laws that may apply.
                            Specifications are subject to change without notice for ongoing product modifications and
                            improvements.
